doc_affix_basic_desc: Standard usage of the Affix component.
doc_affix_best_practice_1: Use for pinning vital interface elements like navigation bars, action buttons, or table of contents, ensuring they remain accessible during scrolling.
doc_affix_best_practice_2: Avoid overusing Affix, as having too many fixed elements can clutter the viewport and diminish the space available for main content.
doc_affix_best_practice_3: Ensure that the fixed element does not obscure other important information or overlap with interactive components like headers or footers.
doc_affix_bottom_desc: Use the offsetBottom property to fix content relative to the bottom of the screen.
doc_affix_long_desc: Details and usage for the Affix component, which fixes an element to a specific position.
doc_affix_design_intent: Affix is used to keep specific elements in a fixed position on the screen according to scrolling. It ensures that important action buttons, navigation, or tables of contents (TOC) always remain in the user's view, improving convenience.
doc_affix_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the purpose of fixed display and the target scope.
doc_affix_choice_affix_when: When you want to temporarily fix part of the content and keep it accessible after scrolling.
doc_affix_choice_sticky_when: When always fixing a specific layout block (header, sidebar, etc.) to the top or side of the page.
doc_affix_choice_anchor_when: When you want to provide navigation for jumping to specific sections within a page.
doc_affix_anatomy_container: Container: The area monitored for scrolling. Usually the entire document, but a specific element can also be specified.
doc_affix_anatomy_element: Fixed Element: The actual following content itself (button, card, list, etc.).
doc_affix_content_guidelines: Carefully select the elements to fix so they do not obscure the main content of the screen. Using multiple affixes simultaneously is not recommended as it reduces the effective display area.
doc_affix_motion_spec: Maintains smooth following animation without jitters at the moment of switching to the fixed state or when the fix is released.
doc_affix_a11y_spec: Ensure that the fixed element does not obscure other focus targets when a keyboard user moves focus. It is important to set offset values appropriately.
doc_affix_scenario_toc: Document Sites: Fixing a table of contents to the right side of long articles to facilitate understanding current location and section movement.
doc_affix_scenario_cta: Product Pages: Fixing a "Buy Now" button to the bottom of the screen to enable conversion actions from any position.
doc_affix_scenario_stats: Dashboards: Ensuring that top-level totals or aggregation criteria are always referencable while scrolling to check detailed data.
doc_anchor_accessibility_desc: Each anchor item is assigned appropriate ARIA attributes for screen reader accessibility.
doc_anchor_basic_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Anchor component.
doc_anchor_title: A component for smooth scrolling to specific sections
doc_anchor_tracking_desc: Monitors scroll position to automatically activate the corresponding anchor item.
doc_anchor_visibility_desc: The anchor link highlights the active item based on the current scroll position.
doc_anchor_design_intent: The Anchor component provides quick access to specific content areas (sections) within a page. It is intended to help users understand their current location on a long page and navigate to their destination without stress.
doc_anchor_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate navigation method based on the page structure and content type.
doc_anchor_choice_anchor_when: When you have many sections on a single page and want them to function like a table of contents.
doc_anchor_choice_nav_when: When you want to move between different pages across the site rather than staying on a single page.
doc_anchor_choice_tabs_when: When you want to switch between content rather than displaying it all at once.
doc_anchor_anatomy_item: Anchor Item: A link to each section. Includes visual emphasis for the active state.
doc_anchor_anatomy_ink: Indicator: A visual guide (vertical line or dot) that indicates the currently active item.
doc_anchor_content_guidelines: Anchor text should match the heading of the target section. Keep it short and concise (e.g., 1-3 words).
doc_anchor_motion_spec: Smoothly scrolls to the destination upon clicking, with the active indicator following smoothly.
doc_anchor_a11y_spec: Each link is keyboard-focusable, and scrolling is triggered by the Enter key. Uses `aria-current` to indicate the current location.
doc_anchor_scenario_toc: Technical Documentation: Jump to specific API descriptions or sections from a table of contents in the sidebar.
doc_anchor_scenario_nav: Product Landing Page: Placed under the hero section to guide users to key sections like 'Features', 'Pricing', and 'FAQ'.
doc_anchor_scenario_article: Long-form Article: Placed at the beginning as 'Table of Contents' to allow readers to start from where they want.
doc_backtop_design_intent: The BackTop component is used to allow users viewing long content to return to the top of the page with a single click. It serves as an auxiliary tool to save scrolling time and improve navigation efficiency.
doc_backtop_choice_matrix_desc: Consider the optimal navigation based on page length and user behavior patterns.
doc_backtop_choice_backtop_when: On vertically long pages where a quick return to the top is needed during or after reading.
doc_backtop_choice_sticky_when: When you want to keep navigation like a global menu visible at all times.
doc_backtop_choice_pagination_when: When physically splitting content to avoid generating long scrolls in the first place.
doc_backtop_anatomy_button: Floating Button: An interactive element fixed at the bottom right of the screen.
doc_backtop_anatomy_icon: Icon: A symbol like an upward arrow that intuitively suggests the action.
doc_backtop_content_guidelines: Place only icons or very concise labels like "TOP" inside the button. It is important to minimize visual noise.
doc_backtop_motion_spec: Fades in when a certain scroll position is reached, and moves to the top with a smooth scroll animation upon clicking.
doc_backtop_a11y_spec: Assign aria-label="Back to page top" to the button to clearly convey the purpose to screen readers.
doc_backtop_scenario_article: Articles & Terms: Vertically long text content like blog posts or terms of use.
doc_backtop_scenario_feed: Infinite Scroll: Dynamic pages where items increase as you scroll, such as product lists or timelines.
doc_backtop_scenario_dashboard: Admin Panels: Pages where many widgets or data tables are arranged, making it difficult to grasp the whole at once.
doc_backtop_best_practice_1: Use on pages with long content where scrolling is significant (typically 3-4 screens deep).
doc_backtop_best_practice_2: Ensure the button's position does not overlap with other floating elements like chat icons or persistent action buttons.
doc_backtop_best_practice_3: Standard placement is the bottom-right corner, aligning with user expectations for universal design patterns.
doc_backtop_custom_desc: You can customize the content of the BackTop button.
doc_backtop_desc: A button that appears when the page is scrolled and allows the user to quickly return to the top.
doc_backtop_target_desc: You can specify a target container for the scroll event.
doc_backtop_usage_1: When the page is very long and needs a quick way to return to the top.
doc_backtop_usage_2: When the user needs to frequently scroll back to the top for navigation or other actions.
doc_navbar_basic_usage_desc: Place components like Navbar.Brand and Navbar.Content inside the Navbar component.
doc_navbar_content_placement_desc: Flexible layouts are possible by combining multiple Navbar.Content components with the justify property (start, center, end).
doc_navbar_desc_2: It functions as a container for brand logos, navigation links, action buttons, etc.
doc_navbar_glass_desc: Use the glass property to apply a background blur effect (frosted glass effect). Highly effective when placed as a fixed header over colourful background images or gradients.
doc_navbar_layout_desc: Flexible layouts are possible by using multiple `Navbar.Content` components and combining the `justify` property (`start`, `center`, `end`).
doc_navbar_long_desc: Navbar is a responsive navigation header placed at the top of the page.
doc_pagination_cons_1_desc: Emphasise the active button so the current page is immediately clear.
doc_pagination_cons_2_desc: Assign appropriate aria-labels to each button (e.g., "Next", "Previous", "Page X").
doc_pagination_cons_3_desc: Use ellipses (...) when there are many pages to avoid displaying all page buttons.
doc_pagination_cons_4_desc: Include "Previous" and "Next" buttons to facilitate sequential page movement. Disable buttons on the first and last pages.
doc_pagination_feat_all_in_one_desc: All features can be used simultaneously.
doc_pagination_feat_quick_jumper_desc: Enable showQuickJumper to display an input field for direct jumps to a specific page.
doc_pagination_feat_simple_desc: The simple property switches to a compact display format, showing "Current Page / Total Pages" instead of page numbers.
doc_pagination_feat_size_changer_desc: Enable showSizeChanger to allow users to change the number of items displayed per page.
doc_pagination_feat_size_changer_props_1: showSizeChanger - Whether to show the page size selector
doc_pagination_feat_size_changer_props_2: pageSizeOptions - Array of selectable page sizes (default: [10, 20, 50, 100])
doc_pagination_feat_size_changer_props_3: onPageSizeChange - Callback for when the page size is changed
doc_pagination_feat_total_display_desc: Use showTotal to display the total count and the range of items currently being shown.
doc_pagination_onchange_change_desc: The onChange callback now receives both the page number and the page size:
doc_pagination_onchange_change_desc_2: This allows the same callback to handle changes in page size.
doc_pagination_var_interactive_desc: A live example where you can actually change pages.
doc_pagination_var_many_pages_desc: Ellipses are automatically displayed when there are many pages.
doc_pagination_var_sibling_desc: Adjust the number of pages displayed before and after the current page using the siblingCount property.
doc_stepper_b_cont_desc: For many steps (5+). Perceived as a single flow, reducing the perceived burden.
doc_stepper_b_hor_desc: Suitable for wide screens like desktops, following the natural eye movement from left to right.
doc_stepper_b_linear_desc: Ensures data integrity in processes where the previous step must be completed (e.g., checkout).
doc_stepper_b_non_desc: Suitable for processes editable in any order (settings, profile, etc.).
doc_stepper_b_sep_desc: For fewer steps (3–4). Emphasizes the independence of each stage.
doc_stepper_b_ver_desc: Ideal for mobile environments or many steps. Makes it easier to expand detailed information.
doc_stepper_c_needs_avoid: Basic operations that are intuitively understood (posting, uploading, etc.)
doc_stepper_c_needs_reco: When complex procedures with a fixed order are required.
doc_stepper_c_steps_avoid: Simple flows with 3 or fewer steps (Input → Confirm → Done)
doc_stepper_c_steps_reco: 4 or more steps (registration, checkout, detailed surveys, etc.)
doc_stepper_r_fb_desc: Clearly indicates Completed, Current, and Pending states, giving a sense of steady progress.
doc_stepper_r_fear_desc: Visualizes "how much more until completion," reducing the user’s psychological burden.
doc_stepper_r_task_desc: Divides complex form inputs into manageable units, increasing completion rates while maintaining focus.
doc_stepper_title: A component that visually displays current progress
doc_stepper_var_err_desc: Indicates that an error occurred in a specific step.
doc_stepper_var_icon_desc: Custom icons can be displayed instead of standard numbers.
doc_stepper_var_inter_desc: An example where steps can actually be advanced or reversed.
doc_stepper_var_lver_desc: Suitable for horizontal layouts where you want to center steps.
doc_stepper_var_ver_desc: Suitable for limited space or when there are many steps.
doc_steps_clickable_desc: By setting `onChange`, users can click each step to change the state.
doc_steps_long_desc: Provides detailed information and usage instructions for the Steps component.
doc_steps_usage_desc: Visually guides a sequence of flows or progress status.
doc_steps_vertical_desc: You can align steps vertically using `direction='vertical'`.
doc_steps_with_icons_desc: Custom icons can be set for each step to enhance visual clarity.
doc_tab_desc: A container to organize related content into switchable view panels
doc_tab_navigation_title: Tabs for navigating within a page or between contexts
doc_tab_title: A container to organize related content into switchable view panels
doc_tabs_title: Tab elements for switching between related blocks of information arranged in panels
doc_tabs_design_intent: Tabs organize related but mutually exclusive blocks of content into panels, allowing users to quickly toggle and browse information within a limited display area.
doc_tabs_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the content being toggled and the level of navigation.
doc_tabs_choice_tabs_when: Suitable for toggling how information is presented within the same page (e.g., Details, History, Settings).
doc_tabs_choice_nav_when: Suitable for navigation that moves between entire page contexts or changes the URL (e.g., Main Menu).
doc_tabs_choice_segmented_when: Suitable for toggling data display formats (e.g., Week, Month, Year) with a small number of choices (2 to 5).
doc_tabs_anatomy_list: Tab List: The container for arranging tab triggers. Has role='tablist'.
doc_tabs_anatomy_trigger: Tab Trigger: Button element for calling up each panel. Has role='tab'.
doc_tabs_anatomy_panel: Tab Panel: Content area corresponding to the selected tab. Has role='tabpanel'.
doc_tabs_content_labels: Keep tab labels short (1 to 2 words) and descriptive of the content.
doc_tabs_motion_slider: The indicator highlighting the active tab slides smoothly, naturally guiding the user's eye movement.
doc_tabs_a11y_keyboard_nav: Navigate between tabs using the left and right arrow keys; toggle display automatically or with the Enter/Space key.
doc_tabs_a11y_focus: The association between the currently active tab and panel is maintained even when focus is lost.
doc_tabs_scenario_profile: User Profile: Toggling between Basic Info, Post List, and Followers List.
doc_tabs_scenario_config: Product Settings: Categorizing General Settings, Security, and Notification Settings.
doc_tabbar_badge_desc: You can display notification counts or indicators by adding the `badge` property to `TabBar.Item`.
doc_tabbar_basic_usage_desc: Use `TabBar.Item` to define each tab. Set icons and labels, and switch the active tab with the `onClick` event.
doc_tabbar_icon_only_desc: Labels are optional. If the label is omitted, the icon is centered vertically.
doc_tabbar_long_desc_p1: TabBar is a navigation bar fixed at the bottom of the screen for mobile applications. It is used to switch between primary screens.
doc_tabbar_mobile_only: Mobile Only: This component is primarily designed for mobile devices (touch operation, small screens). For desktop environments, consider using `Sidebar` or `Navbar`.
doc_tabnavigation_basic_desc: The default style is `underline`. This is commonly used for secondary page navigation.
doc_tabnavigation_contained_desc: The Contained style is similar to a SegmentedControl, often used for changing data display formats (e.g., switching the time axis of a chart).
doc_tabnavigation_icons_desc: You can add an icon to each item using the `icon` property.
doc_tabnavigation_long_desc: TabNavigation provides a set of links for navigating between different views or contexts. Unlike the `Tabs` component, it is designed primarily for page-level navigation and filtering, typically involving URL transitions.
doc_tabnavigation_pill_desc: The Pill style is suitable for operations that do not drastically change the context, such as filtering a list or toggling a state.
doc_tabs_a11y_1: Supports keyboard arrow-key navigation between tab list items.
doc_tabs_a11y_2: Root element uses role='tablist'; items use role='tab'.
doc_tabs_a11y_3: Content containers are identified via role='tabpanel'.
doc_tabs_a11y_4: Associations managed automatically via aria-labelledby and aria-controls.
doc_tabs_content_desc: The container area where specific tab details are rendered.
doc_tabs_full_width_desc: Enable fixed width to distribute tabs equally across the entire horizontal space.
doc_tabs_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Tabs component.
doc_tabs_scrollable_desc: Enable horizontal scrolling for tab lists that exceed container width.
doc_tabs_size_variations_desc: Control the size of the tab elements using tokens like 'sm', 'md', or 'lg'.
doc_tabs_state_management_desc: The Tabs component supports both controlled and uncontrolled operational patterns.
doc_tabs_trigger_desc: Individual clickable elements that switch the active tab content.
doc_tabs_type_scrollable_purpose: Best for deep hierarchies or many categories
doc_tabs_ui_ux_desc: User experience considerations for efficient tab switching.
doc_tabs_vertical_alignment_desc: How to align tab triggers vertically within side panels.
doc_tabs_with_icons_desc: Combine text with icons for improved visual identification and cleaner UI.
doc_breadcrumb_custom_separator_desc: Customize the separator character using the `separator` property.
doc_breadcrumb_long_desc: A navigation component that helps users understand their current location and easily return to higher-level pages.
doc_breadcrumb_design_intent: Breadcrumbs are used to help users understand their current location within a site's hierarchical structure and to quickly return to higher levels. They are especially helpful in deep sites for maintaining information context.
doc_breadcrumb_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal navigation based on the site structure and user movement paths.
doc_breadcrumb_choice_breadcrumb_when: When showing the overall hierarchical structure (directory) of the site in order and indicating the path the user has taken.
doc_breadcrumb_choice_tabs_when: When switching between different views within the same page or the same hierarchy level.
doc_breadcrumb_choice_anchor_when: When jumping between specific sections within a long page.
doc_breadcrumb_anatomy_link: Link Item: A link to a higher hierarchy level. Clicking it allows the user to return to that level.
doc_breadcrumb_anatomy_separator: Separator: A character dividing items. Visually indicates the depth of the hierarchy.
doc_breadcrumb_anatomy_current: Current Page: Located at the end of the list, indicating the currently displayed location. Usually has no link.
doc_breadcrumb_content_guidelines: Keep item labels concise and consistent. It is generally recommended to match the target page title.
doc_breadcrumb_motion_spec: Visually indicate interactivity by displaying underlines on hover or changing link colors.
doc_breadcrumb_a11y_spec: Assign aria-label="Breadcrumb" to the nav element and structure it with ol/li elements. The current page must have aria-current="page" set.
doc_breadcrumb_scenario_ec: E-commerce Site: Tracing category hierarchies (Home > Electronics > TV) to find a target product.
doc_breadcrumb_scenario_files: File Management: Returning to upper levels from deep folder hierarchies (Documents > Project > 2024).
doc_breadcrumb_scenario_admin: Admin Panel: Returning to a list from details of setting items (User Settings > Security > Two-Step Verification).
doc_breadcrumb_usage_desc: Pass an array of labels and links to the `items` property.
doc_breadcrumb_with_icons_desc: Icons can be displayed for each item.
